overview.title: Overview
overview.lead: CAOS LDA HSI explores what probabilistic topic models can capture when documents are pixels, regions, or measurements drawn from hyperspectral imagery.

hero.title: We treat every hyperspectral pixel as a document.
hero.caption: Each curve is the mean signature of one agronomic class in the Indian Pines cube. The inter-class diversity at 1100 / 1400 / 1900 nm — water and feldspar absorptions — is what the textual corpus has to recover discretely. That signature is a document; the project's question is which topics (shared latent structure) live in it.
hero.datacube_caption: An HSI cube is (H × W × B). Each pixel x[i, j] is a B-band spectral vector. The project walks it as a corpus, discretises it into tokens, fits topics θ ∈ Δ^(K-1) and compares them against K-dim baselines.

pipeline_anatomy.title: From the HSI cube to θ ∈ Δ^(K-1)
pipeline_anatomy.lead: Four steps that the app serves pre-computed behind every /api/topic-views/{scene} endpoint: define document, discretise to tokens, fit the model, inspect the per-pixel θ mixture.
pipeline_anatomy.stage1_note: x ∈ ℝᴮ per pixel · region · sample

scenes.title: Each scene with its class distribution
scenes.lead: Each bar is one agronomic / urban / mineral class with its real relative frequency. KSC and Botswana are tough benchmarks (diluted majority class, low SNR).

pillars.title: Any topic result is attributable to these three levers
pillars.documents_body: How to build a document: by pixel, by region (SLIC, Felzenszwalb), by patch or by full sample. Four alternatives materialised in build_groupings; they change the statistical unit upstream.
pillars.discretisation_body: How a continuous spectrum becomes a discrete token: twelve recipes V1..V12, three quantisation schemes (uniform, quantile, equalised), three levels Q ∈ {8, 16, 32}.
pillars.model_body: Canonical LDA (collapsed Gibbs, variational Bayes, online), HDP, CTM, ProdLDA, ETM, NMF, PCA, ICA, dense-AE, CAE-1D/2D/3D, β-VAE — all at the same K for a fair comparison.

coverage.title: 11 + 5 + 4 + 12 axes — all evaluated on the same scene when applicable

reading_path.title: Suggested path — 7 stops
reading_path.step1_body: The PTM/LDA model and why an HSI pixel can be treated as a document.
reading_path.step2_body: The V1..V12 × schemes × Q grid plus 5 deep encoders.
reading_path.step3_body: Which scripts run locally to produce the material this app serves.
reading_path.step4_body: How topics are applied to classification and regression: direct / routed / embedded.
reading_path.step5_body: The 21 datasets with their access path, local root and source files.
reading_path.step6_body: Guided flow family → subset → representation → 11 tabs per labelled scene.
reading_path.step7_body: 16 cards: multi-axis battery, Bayesian HDI, K-curve, β-collapse, neural-topic head-to-head, …

overview.manifest_loaded: Live data layer — manifest loaded correctly.
overview.manifest_static: Numbers are from the repo manifest — the app serves pre-computed material, no in-browser fitting.

index.lead: Four entries: the theory behind the model, the representations the corpus accepts, the pipeline that produces the data, and how topics are applied to downstream tasks.
index.reading: Suggested reading: Theory → Representations → Pipeline → Application. The Bayesian Method Comparison and Multi-Axis Addendum B wiki pages close the methodological loop.
index.theory_body: The probabilistic topic model (PTM) and why an HSI pixel can be treated as a document. LDA in plate notation, variational inference, generalisations to HDP and CTM.
index.repr_body: Twelve wordification recipes (V1 band → V12 GMM-token), three schemes (uniform/quantile/equalised), Q ∈ {8, 16, 32}. Plus five deep encoders: CAE-1D / CAE-2D / CAE-3D anchor and full-patch / β-VAE. PCA, NMF, ICA as fair K-dim baselines.
index.pipe_body: Diagram of the 12 stages of the data-pipeline: fetch_* (acquire) → research_core (paths/loaders) → build_* (offline compute) → curate_for_web → manifest. Five torch builders detect GPU automatically with CPU fallback.
index.app_body: How topics are applied to downstream tasks: flat feature (theta_logistic, dominated by raw), gating (topic_routed_soft = soft mixture of per-topic specialists, matches or beats raw 6/6), embedded (concat [θ | pca_K], small effect on IP). The B-3 follow-up closes: the Dirichlet simplex matters, not K-dim compression.

methodology_representations.title: Representations · method-by-method
methodology_representations.lead: Each method (16 total — 5 classical topic models + 2 neural topic models + 4 K-dim baselines + 5 deep encoders + 1 unmixing) has its own theoretical formulation, design principles, starting hypothesis, and findings against the 6 labelled scenes. Pick a method to drill in.
methodology_representations.recipes_lead: Topic-family methods operate on a discrete word alphabet. The project ships 12 wordification recipes (V1..V12) × 3 quantisation schemes × Q ∈ {8, 16, 32} = 108 configs per scene. The grid below sketches the space. The canonical recipe is V1 (band-frequency, band-as-word) with uniform Q=8.
methodology_representations.recipes_figure_caption: 12 recipes × 9 (3 schemes × 3 Q) = 108 configs per scene.

lda.label: LDA · online VB
lda.theory_body: Latent Dirichlet Allocation models each document as a mixture θ_d over K topics. Each token w_{d,n} is generated by first drawing a topic z_{d,n} with probability θ_d, then a word with probability φ_{z_{d,n}}. Online variational inference (Hoffman–Blei–Bach 2010) uses mini-batches and stochastic VI.
principles: K is fixed a priori (K = n_classes by project convention).
Canonical priors from the Procemin/A39 paper: α = 0.45, η = 0.2.
Canonical recipe V1 (band-frequency, band-as-word) with Q ∈ {8, 16, 32}.
lda.hypothesis: If a hyperspectral image is a corpus of pixels-as-documents, the discrete topics discovered should align with labelled agronomic/mineral classes.
lda.findings: Wins ARI on 4/6 scenes (Indian Pines, Salinas, Salinas-A, Pavia U) vs ProdLDA and ETM in head-to-head; loses 1/6 in KSC where it collapses to ARI≈0; loses 1/6 in Botswana vs ETM. Coherence c_v 0/6 (always worst). σ ≤ 0.03 ARI over N=5 seeds — extremely stable.

lda_tomo.label: LDA · tomotopy (collapsed Gibbs)
lda_tomo.theory_body: Collapsed: integrates φ and θ analytically and leaves only the discrete topic assignments z as Gibbs variables. Iteration i: re-sample z_i conditional on the rest. Slower (10× online VB) but usually finds better local maxima.
principles: No variational gap — the sampler distribution converges to the true posterior.
Burn-in + N effective iterations; per-document log-likelihood as diagnostic.
C++ implementation via tomotopy → 50× faster than pure-Python Gibbs.
lda_tomo.hypothesis: A better φ estimator should translate to more semantically coherent topics (higher c_v).
lda_tomo.findings: Wins coherence c_v on 4/6 scenes vs online LDA. Coherence-vs-ARI tradeoff: tomotopy is strong on c_v, but ARI vs label stays similar. Use it when interpretability is the priority.

lda_sparse.label: LDA · sparse VB
lda_sparse.theory_body: Same as online LDA but with α = 0.05 (Dirichlet mode pushed towards simplex vertices). θ_d becomes peaked: each document concentrates on few topics.
principles: Strict sparsity prior: documents with only 1–3 active topics.
Useful when classes are disjoint (agronomic classes don't mix within a pixel).
Perplexity usually worse — but better fit for categorical labels.
lda_sparse.hypothesis: Higher sparsity should improve ARI vs label (every document → one clear dominant topic).
lda_sparse.findings: Worse perplexity than canonical LDA, marginally worse ARI in most scenes. Confirms that for HSI data, pixels are not single-topic.

hdp.label: HDP · tomotopy
hdp.theory_body: HDP (Teh–Jordan–Beal–Blei 2006) is the non-parametric extension of LDA: the number of topics K is learned from the corpus via stick-breaking. Topics are shared across documents via a hierarchical Dirichlet process.
principles: K is not fixed a priori — the model decides how many active topics exist.
Truncation T (upper cap) for tractable inference; T = 50 typical.
Useful when there's no prior on n_classes (unlabelled scenes).
hdp.hypothesis: For scenes with unknown class count, HDP should auto-identify the effective K and converge to coherent topics.
hdp.findings: On the 6 labelled scenes, HDP typically activates 8–15 effective topics (n_classes ∈ {9, 13, 16}). c_v comparable to LDA. ARI vs label competitive with LDA when the learned K is close to the true one.

ctm.label: CTM · tomotopy
ctm.theory_body: Blei–Lafferty 2007. Replaces the Dirichlet prior with a logistic-normal: η_d ∈ ℝ^{K-1} with non-diagonal covariance Σ. Softmax converts η to θ. Σ encodes topic correlations.
principles: Relaxes the topic independence that LDA imposes via the Dirichlet.
Inference more expensive (logistic-normal is not conjugate with multinomial).
Σ is an additional free parameter with K(K-1)/2 entries.
ctm.hypothesis: For HSI, spectrally neighbouring topics may correlate (e.g. early- and late-stage vegetation share bands). CTM should capture this.
ctm.findings: Compute time 5–10× LDA. Marginal ARI gain (<0.02). Off-diagonal Σ is not structurally informative on the tested corpora.

prodlda.label: ProdLDA · Pyro
prodlda.theory_body: Neural topic model: an MLP encoder amortises z inference, the decoder is a multinomial softmax over β = K×V. ELBO with Dirichlet KL via Laplace approximation.
principles: Neural encoder + decoder — gradient training over the full corpus.
Topic coherence c_v typically better than LDA (the decoder learns band-relations).
Init-sensitive: σ(ARI) ≈ 0.03 across N=5 seeds.
prodlda.hypothesis: Amortised encoder should match LDA on ARI and improve c_v thanks to the neural decoder.
prodlda.findings: Wins c_v 6/6 scenes vs LDA and ETM. Loses ARI 4/6 vs LDA (5/6 vs ETM). Confirms the explicit tradeoff: coherence vs discriminability. Operational rule: ProdLDA for interpretability, LDA for clustering.

etm.label: ETM · Embedded Topic Model
etm.theory_body: Factorised decoder: β = ρα^T where ρ ∈ ℝ^{V×E} are word embeddings and α ∈ ℝ^{K×E} are topic embeddings. E ≪ V accelerates and regularises. Variational gaussian over θ.
principles: Low-rank decoder E ≤ 256 — captures semantic similarity between words (neighbouring bands).
Word embeddings ρ are learned jointly with topics.
Better for large vocabularies; HSI has V ≈ 200 bands.
etm.hypothesis: A shared word embedding should help when there's spectral redundancy (correlated bands).
etm.findings: ETM > ProdLDA on ARI 5/6 scenes (ProdLDA wins only on KSC). Coherence c_v in the middle: ProdLDA > ETM > LDA. ETM is the best ARI+c_v compromise.

nmf.label: NMF · K=8
nmf.theory_body: Non-negative matrix factorisation. β-divergence = KL. W ∈ ℝ_+^{N×K} are per-document scores, H ∈ ℝ_+^{K×V} are basis spectra. Multiplicative update rules.
principles: Parts-based: every spectrum is a non-negative sum of K basis spectra.
No stochastic priors — deterministic solution (init-dependent).
Direct K-dim baseline against LDA / PCA / AE at the same K.
nmf.hypothesis: If spectral mixing is physically linear, NMF should recover endmembers comparable to NFINDR.
nmf.findings: Reconstruction RMSE comparable to PCA at K=8. Silhouette and ARI vs label worse than PCA on 4/6 scenes — NMF components are more interpretable than separable.

pca.label: PCA · K=8
pca.theory_body: Eigendecomposition of the covariance C ∈ ℝ^{V×V}. Top-K eigenvectors form the basis V_{:K}. The projection z_d = V_{:K}^T x_d is L2-optimal in reconstruction (Eckart–Young).
principles: Linear compression — preserves maximum variance in K directions.
Reconstruction RMSE is minimal at every K (its only guaranteed title).
Components can be negative — no direct physical interpretation.
pca.hypothesis: For HSI data dominated by a single brightness direction of variance, PCA should be a hard baseline to beat on MSE but poor on separability.
pca.findings: Wins reconstruction RMSE at every tested K (4, 8, 12, 16, 32). Silhouette intermediate. ARI vs label moderate. It is the canonical L2 baseline against which every deep encoder must justify itself.

ica.label: ICA · K=8 (FastICA)
ica.theory_body: Independent Component Analysis. Assumes x = As with statistically independent (non-Gaussian) sources s. FastICA uses kurtosis / negentropy. ν ~ N(0,1) is the Gaussian reference.
principles: Statistical independence > decorrelation (PCA).
Useful when distinguishable physical sources exist (water, vegetation, soil).
Slower convergence than PCA but comparable cost overall.
ica.hypothesis: If HSI is a mixture of few independent physical sources, ICA should recover them better than PCA in silhouette.
ica.findings: Latents comparable to PCA in ARI/NMI. Silhouette slightly lower on 3/6 scenes — ICA components capture different structure but not necessarily more separable.

dense_ae.label: Dense AE · K=8
dense_ae.theory_body: Linear neural baseline. MLP encoder → bottleneck K, symmetric MLP decoder. MSE loss. No convolution, no recurrence. Equivalent to non-linear PCA.
principles: Non-linear generalisation of PCA at the same K.
GPU-trained (project cycles 59+).
No additional regularisation (no KL, no dropout) — comparable to PCA in degenerate cases.
dense_ae.hypothesis: For low K (4, 8), non-linear relations between bands should give better reconstruction than PCA.
dense_ae.findings: Reconstruction RMSE comparable to PCA (not better at K=8 averaged over 6 scenes). Silhouette and ARI slightly worse than PCA — the model learns the same as PCA but with more seed variance.

cae_1d.label: CAE-1D · K=8
cae_1d.theory_body: 1D convolutional AE over the spectrum. Kernel = 7 adjacent bands. Encoder = 3 Conv1D layers + 2 dense, bottleneck K = 8.
principles: Assumes local structure in the spectrum (neighbouring bands correlated).
Receptive field grows exponentially with depth — captures wider features.
Stride 2 between layers progressively reduces the spectral dimension.
cae_1d.hypothesis: Hyperspectral spectra have band-to-band autocorrelation. CAE-1D should capture local features (edges, peaks) better than PCA/dense AE.
cae_1d.findings: Better seed stability (ARI std 15–20% lower than LDA on 5/6 scenes). Silhouette comparable to PCA but with lower σ. Default deep-compression option when stability is the goal.

cae_2d.label: CAE-2D · K=8 (anchor)
cae_2d.theory_body: 2D CAE over P×P×B patches (P=7 spatial pixels × B bands). Conv2D encoder + bottleneck K=8 just for the centre pixel of the patch.
principles: Captures spatial texture (not just the spectral profile).
Anchor mode: only the patch centre contributes to loss and to z.
Spatial neighbourhood = 3.5 pixels effective radius.
cae_2d.hypothesis: Spatial context should improve separability — an agricultural pixel surrounded by agricultural pixels is easier to classify.
cae_2d.findings: At N=15 seeds: ARI vs label competitive with CAE-1D. Better on scenes with clear textures (Indian Pines), worse on fine mosaics (Salinas-A).

cae_3d.label: CAE-3D anchor · K=8
cae_3d.theory_body: Conv3D over full cubes (spatial × spectral dimension). 3D kernels = (3 bands × 3 px × 3 px). Anchor: only the centre pixel defines z.
principles: Volumetric receptive field — captures spatial and spectral neighbourhood simultaneously.
More compute-expensive — GPU training 50–120× via CUDA.
Anchor mode reduces loss noise by focusing on the target pixel.
cae_3d.hypothesis: Conv3D should be strictly better than CAE-2D + CAE-1D combined — more native inductive capacity.
cae_3d.findings: K-curve {K=4, K=8} neutral: ΔARI K=4 = +0.011 (5/6 scenes confirm direction); Pavia U inverts with capacity. Not a decisive gain over CAE-1D — volumetric capacity is not the dominant lever.

cae_3d_full.label: CAE-3D full · K=8
cae_3d_full.theory_body: Same net as CAE-3D anchor, but the loss penalises every pixel of the P×P patch (not only the centre). Uses more signal per gradient step.
principles: Dense loss (P² more pixels contribute) reduces gradient noise.
K-dim embedding per pixel computed at inference.
GPU-trained; same architecture as anchor, different loss.
cae_3d_full.hypothesis: Dense loss should give cleaner gradients and more stable latents.
cae_3d_full.findings: Equivalent to anchor on ARI vs label at N=15 across 6 scenes. Not decisively better on any scene. Cycle 58 confirmed identical K-curve between anchor and full.

beta_vae.label: β-VAE · K=8
beta_vae.tag: Higgins et al. 2017 · β KL term
beta_vae.theory_body: Variational AE with β multiplier on the KL term. β = 1 ⇒ standard VAE. β > 1 forces z dimensions to be more independent (disentangled).
principles: Probabilistic latent — z is a distribution, not a point.
β controls the reconstruction-vs-disentanglement tradeoff.
Canonical default here: β = 2.
beta_vae.hypothesis: A disentangled latent should help interpretability without sacrificing much reconstruction.
beta_vae.findings: At β = 2, comparable to CAE-1D on ARI/NMI. K-curve sweep β ∈ {1, 2, 8, 16} shows documented collapse at β ≥ 8 (KL → 0, only the prior remains). β = 2 is the sweet spot.

endmember.label: Endmembers · NFINDR + NNLS
endmember.theory_body: NFINDR (Winter 1999): K endmembers are the vertices of the maximum-volume simplex in the HSI cube. NNLS with sum-to-one constraint (delta = 100 penalty) infers per-pixel abundance.
principles: Linear mixing model — every pixel = convex sum of K endmembers.
Physically interpretable: every endmember is a candidate pure material.
Compared against LDA via topic_endmember_match: K-K cosine matrix.
endmember.hypothesis: If HSI pixels are linear combinations of few pure materials, LDA topics should align with endmembers.
endmember.findings: Best per-topic cosine vs endmember typically 0.7–0.9 (high alignment) on mineral scenes. On agricultural (non-mineral) scenes the alignment drops to 0.4–0.6 — confirming that LDA recovers additional non-linear structure.

methodology_application.title: Application to classification and regression via topics

databases.title: Databases
databases.lead: 21 datasets grouped in 4 families. Each declares modality, domains, supervision status, and whether it has local roots available for the pipeline. Raw data is not served from the web app — only the publishable derived artifacts.
databases.family_a_tagline: USGS / ECOSTRESS curves — reference spectra with label or measurement.
databases.family_b_tagline: AVIRIS / ROSIS / Hyperion cubes with pixel-level labels — Indian Pines, Salinas, Pavia U, KSC, Botswana.
databases.family_cd_tagline: HIDSAG geo-mining + MicaSense MSI — regions with physical or geochemical measurements.
databases.family_e_tagline: Cubes without reliable labels — Borsoi unmixing benchmarks (Samson, Jasper Ridge, Urban) with manual endmembers.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.lead: Guided flow in four steps: family → subset → representation → explore. Each step depends on the previous; you can go back at any time.

dataset_overview.not_found: No entry found in the inventory for {{id}}.
dataset_overview.lead: This subset has no derived-analysis artifacts yet (no LDA, no per-scene EDA, no endmember unmixing). What we know about it comes from the canonical inventory: source, raw files, supervision, license. Topic-model panels open up automatically once this dataset acquires derived artifacts.
dataset_overview.files_lead: First 24 files shown; sizes in MB.

benchmarks.lead: 16+ panels organised by Addendum B axis. Use the tabs to jump to the group you want. URL #hash allows sharing a specific tab.
